---
title: "4.12 2D Array Traversals"
subtitle: "Data Collections"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### 2D traversal uses nested loops

A 2D array traversal usually has:

::: {.tight-list}
- outer loop for rows
- inner loop for columns
- body that uses `array[row][col]`
:::

## Row-Major Traversal

### Visit one full row at a time

```java
for (int row = 0; row < grid.length; row++)
{
    for (int col = 0; col < grid[row].length; col++)
    {
        System.out.println(grid[row][col]);
    }
}
```

This is the most common AP CSA traversal order.

## Column-Major Traversal

### Visit one full column at a time

```java
for (int col = 0; col < grid[0].length; col++)
{
    for (int row = 0; row < grid.length; row++)
    {
        System.out.println(grid[row][col]);
    }
}
```

This assumes a rectangular 2D array.

## Quick Check

### Which order is row-major?

Row-major means:

::: {.tight-list}
- choose a row
- visit each column in that row
- move to the next row
:::

In code, the outer loop is usually `row`, and the inner loop is usually `col`.

## Enhanced For-Each

### Read each row, then each value

```java
for (int[] row : grid)
{
    for (int value : row)
    {
        System.out.println(value);
    }
}
```

For-each is clear for reading all values, but not for changing values by index.

## 2D Array of Objects

### Each cell can store an object reference

![Pixel image](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-12-2d-array-traversals/pixel.jpg){fig-align="center" width="36%"}

Image processing can treat pixels as objects in a 2D grid.

```java
Pixel[][] pixels = picture.getPixels2D();
```

## Groupwork Coding Challenge {.image-fit}

### Picture Lab

![Arch image](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-12-2d-array-traversals/arch.jpg){fig-align="center" width="38%"}

Use nested loops over pixels to modify an image.

Class focus:

::: {.tight-list}
- row and column traversal
- object methods on each `Pixel`
- changing state inside the nested loop
:::

## Classroom Check

### A strong answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- write row-major traversal with nested loops
- write column-major traversal for rectangular arrays
- use `array.length` for rows and `array[row].length` for columns
- explain when enhanced for-each is appropriate
- connect 2D object arrays to image or grid processing
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
